ops.title: Fleet convention for colorized README demo screenshots — bat paints the capture, freeze renders --language ansi; established across 10 repos 2026-07-07
ops.body:
README `docs/assets/demo.png` images across the cc-* fleet are colorized via the demo-pipeline (not product code): `docs/scripts/demo.sh` captures real CLI output to a temp file ending in `.ansi`, paints it with `bat --plain --color=always -l <lang>` (`help` for `--help` output, `yaml` for status boards / key-value stats, `bash` for the leading `$ cmd` prompt line, per-line painting for mixed transcripts), then renders with `freeze <capture> --language ansi` keeping each repo's original theme/window/padding/font flags and pngquant tails. Established 2026-07-07 across cc-context, cc-interact, cc-merge, cc-notes, cc-orchestrate, cc-squash, cc-steer, cc-transcript, cc-pool (claude-pool checkout; `ccp --help` only — richer surfaces print real account emails), and cc-sentiment (new script).

**Why:** the CLIs emit no ANSI (`CLICOLOR_FORCE` does nothing), and freeze's theme only styles window chrome — a freeze of plain text is grayscale regardless of theme. User chose pipeline colorization over adding color libraries to ~10 codebases.

**How to apply:** new repos' demo.sh should follow this shape from the start (see any of the repos above for reference). Verify a recolor with size `< 1 MiB`, saturation `magick <png> -colorspace HSL -channel G -separate +channel -format '%[fx:mean]' info:` well above 0.02, AND a visual read — the dark `#0d1117` background alone measures ~0.28, so saturation can't distinguish gray-on-dark from colored; the visual check is the real gate. Related: [[repo-images-under-1mib]].
